context_note: England vs Croatia. FTR (vig-adj): England 58.8% / Draw 24.8% / Croatia 16.4%. Draw price 24.8% is borderline (just under the 25% RULE8 threshold for extra shrinkage on count-comparison props) - noted but not triggered. England a clear moderate favorite. Croatia is a decent side (not a minnow like Curacao/Egypt) with a 2022 WC semi-final pedigree, but England's stats have been strong in recent tune-ups (Costa Rica 8-SOT game, Japan 11-corner game). KEY DATA: England corners extremely high (5-11 per match, avg 8.4); Croatia fouls consistently high (13-18, avg 13.0); Croatia offsides dominated by one outlier (0,1,0,4,0 - the 4 was vs Belgium). Posture: 'be aggressive on grounded estimates' - Q4, Q6, Q7 are the high-edge low-probability calls where ESPN data strongly supports extremity and crowd will compress toward 50%.

illiquid_unusable: England -0.5 corners / Croatia +0.5 corners (119427167) - zero quotes, completely illiquid

england_n5.note: very consistent low-foul, high-corner, moderate-offside team; one away game (Albania) shows reduced corner output (5 vs ~9.25 home avg)

croatia_n5.note: fouls: 5 vs Slovenia is a low outlier (all others 13-18); offsides: 4 vs Belgium is the sole outlier (others all 0-1). Without respective outliers: fouls avg ~15.0, offsides avg ~0.25

q1_england_more_2h_sot_than_croatia.method: ESPN 2H-scaled Skellam (lambda_ENG=2.8, lambda_CRO=2.0). Raw P=0.642, continuity-corrected P=0.554. RULE7: submit near-raw unshaded. RULE14 check: P(England more 2H SOT)=0.60 > P(England more 2H goals)=0.507 (market-derived) - constraint holds (SOT>=goals). England's SOT avg 5.6 vs Croatia's 4.0 reflects England's recent dominance in attack.

q2_btts_and_3plus_goals.method: Direct Smarkets 'Both teams to score and over/under 2.5' market, Yes/Over contract vig-adjusted 36.73%. Undeviated.

q3_penalty_or_red_card.method: P(penalty)=28.18% (vig-adj liquid Smarkets market). P(red card)~10% (base rate for competitive international; Smarkets 'any player sent off' market has unusual structure with >90% implied red-card rate - likely a booking/yellow-card market mislabeled, excluded). P(A or B)=0.2818+0.10-0.0282=0.354.

q4_england_more_fouls_than_croatia.method: Skellam: England avg=8.2 (n=5, range 7-10, very consistent), Croatia avg=13.0 (n=5, range 5-18). Raw P(ENG>CRO)=0.149. RULE12 sensitivity: Croatia outlier (5-foul game vs Slovenia) - without it Croatia avg=15.0, gap even larger (P even lower ~0.07). England outlier (10-foul game) - without it England avg=7.0, gap again larger. Signal is ROBUST and gets stronger on outlier removal - no RULE12 concern. Draw price 24.8% (barely under 25% RULE8 threshold): no extra shrinkage applied. RULE10 check: England is a moderate (not blowout) favorite, so blowout-regime does not apply. HIGH CONFIDENCE low estimate. Key strategic note: crowd will compress this toward ~0.30-0.35 (per crowd_pred formula), creating the largest potential edge on this match.

q5_kane_1plus_sot_2h.method: Derived from Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' full-match market, Kane contract (liquid two-sided: bid=0.6711/offer=0.6757, mid=0.6734). Question is 2H-specific: P(1+ in 2H) = 1 - exp(-lambda/2) where lambda = -ln(1-0.6734) = 1.119, lambda_2H = 0.560 -> P = 0.4285. Kane is England's primary striker, expected to be on pitch for full match.

q6_croatia_more_corners_than_england_ht.method: ESPN corners: England avg=8.4/match (5,7,11,8,11 - notable: 5 was the only away game; neutral-venue estimate ~7). Croatia avg=4.0/match (3,4,2,3,8 - 8 was the only notable home game; neutral estimate ~4). 1H-scaled lambdas: England=3.5, Croatia=2.0. Skellam raw P(Croatia>England)=0.261. Corner-handicap market completely illiquid (England -0.5 / Croatia +0.5: zero quotes). RULE7: England corners are the single most dominant stat in our data (8.4 avg, all matches 5-11). Even at a neutral venue England should generate far more corners than Croatia. High-edge, well-grounded low estimate.

q7_croatia_2plus_offsides.method: First-pass Poisson: Croatia offsides n=5 (0,1,0,4,0), avg=1.0, lambda=1.0. P(X>=2)=1-e^-1*(1+1)=0.2642. RULE7 unshaded - this is the 7th data point for the '2+ offsides' family (all 6 prior resolved NO, unshaded wins net +54.57/6). OUTLIER NOTE: Croatia's 4-offside game vs Belgium dominates the avg; without it, lambda=0.25 and P(X>=2)=0.026. Large outlier sensitivity (0.026 to 0.264, >10pp swing). However, the outlier makes the estimate HIGHER (not LOWER), and direction is already on NO-side of 50%. Staying unshaded as RULE7 prescribes - the direction (well below 0.5) is unaffected by the sensitivity.

q8_both_teams_1plus_sot_2h.method: ESPN 2H-scaled: P(England 1+ SOT in 2H)=1-e^-2.8=0.939. P(Croatia 1+ SOT in 2H)=1-e^-2.0=0.865. Naive independence product=0.812. RULE4: don't trust a low independence product for this question type (QAT-SUI Q1 showed we can go too low). SAU-URU Q2 caution: when one team takes a lead and sits back, the defending team can get 0 SOT in 2H. Shade from 0.812 to 0.76 for match-script risk (neither team is guaranteed to be attacking in 2H - if England goes up early, Croatia might not create chances, or vice versa). Still well above RULE4's 'don't go too low' guidance.

q9_england_win.method: Direct from Smarkets full-time-result market, vig-adjusted England 58.80%. Treated as tier1_market undeviated per RULE1/2.

q10_sucic_1plus_sot.method: Direct from Smarkets 'Player to have over 0.5 shots on target' market, Luka Sucic contract (offer-only illiquid, mid=45.87%), x0.945 ratio = 0.4335. Full match (no 2H restriction on Q10). Note: separate 'Petar Sucic' contract also exists (14.49%) - confirmed the correct Luka Sucic at 45.87% (attacking midfielder for Croatia, regularly creates and shoots).

crowd_pred_estimates.note: predicted crowd values for out-of-sample formula test

match_facts.note: England won 4-2. High scoring game. England dominated.

Q1_Croatia_HT_more_corners.notes: Croatia did NOT have more HT corners. England's corner dominance confirmed. 2+ offsides family style win.

Q2_Croatia_2plus_offsides.notes: Croatia <2 offsides. 2+ offsides family win (now n+1). Croatia avg 1.0 offsides. Our 26% correct.

Q3_both_teams_1plus_SOT_2H.notes: Both teams had 1+ SOT in 2H. England scored 4 total so YES easily.

Q4_BTTS_3plus_goals.notes: BTTS AND 3+ goals: YES (4-2). Outcome YES, crowd 41% slightly closer than our 37%.

Q5_penalty_or_red_card.notes: Penalty or red card: YES. Tiny edge over crowd.

Q6_England_more_fouls.notes: England did NOT have more fouls. England avg 8.2 fouls vs Croatia 13.0 — our 15% was extremely well-grounded.

Q7_Kane_1plus_SOT_2H.notes: WORST Q this match. SUBMISSION ERROR — model recommended 0.43 (from Smarkets market) but 0.20 was submitted. Kane DID have a 2H SOT. Loss of -44.54 RBP from error.

Q8_England_more_2H_SOT.notes: England more 2H SOT: YES (won 4-2). Tiny edge.

Q9_England_wins.notes: England wins: YES. Both near-identical; slight edge.

Q10_Sucic_1plus_SOT.notes: Sucic 0 SOT. Crowd 34% closer to truth than our 43%. RULE15 should have applied (Croatia losing team).

post_match_results.learning_notes: Q7 Kane (-44.54) was a SUBMISSION ERROR (submitted 0.20 vs model recommendation 0.43). Without error: +45.77 RBP. This is the 2nd-worst single question ever. Q6 England fouls (+18.82) was our best and confirmed low-foul dominant team pattern. Sucic (-3.42) shows RULE15 should apply to expected-loser secondary forwards too.
